summary: Make the Makefile host-cold-test invocation self-terminating (non-interactive, no post-load REPL block) and bound it with a portable timeout watchdog, plus a regression test for the no-hang guarantee.

items.title: host-cold-test must not hang on post-load REPL; add timeout watchdog + regression test
items.description:
Fix the deterministic hang in `make test` -> `host-cold-test`.

ROOT CAUSE: In Makefile target `host-cold-test` (around lines 158-195) the command `$(BUILDDIR)/clamiga --heap 384M --load $(HOST_COLD_TEST_SCRIPT) > $(HOST_COLD_TEST_LOG) 2>&1` runs clamiga in INTERACTIVE mode. main.c (default branch, ~line 573) calls cl_repl() after the --load action completes, and cl_repl() (src/core/repl.c ~line 456) blocks in platform_read_line(). The recipe does not redirect stdin, so under an interactive `make test` stdin is the terminal, EOF never arrives, and the process hangs after the sento suite has finished. The sibling runner trunk/run-load-and-test-all.sh:105 avoids this exact problem with `</dev/null`; the Makefile target does not. None of the trunk/load-and-test-*.lisp scripts self-(quit), so the fix belongs at the invocation layer, not in the shared script.

FIX (Makefile, host-cold-test recipe only):
1. Make the clamiga run non-interactive so it can never enter the REPL after loading: pass the existing `--non-interactive` flag (see main.c arg parsing and the `non_interactive` branch which runs the --load/--eval actions then falls through to `shutdown:` with no cl_repl()). Keep `--heap 384M --load $(HOST_COLD_TEST_SCRIPT)`. For belt-and-suspenders and consistency with run-load-and-test-all.sh, also redirect `</dev/null`.
2. Add a PORTABLE timeout watchdog so a genuine in-suite hang (e.g. a sento dispatcher deadlock or a network fetch in ensure-ql-lib) fails loudly instead of blocking forever - mirroring the host-side watchdog the Makefile comments say the Amiga path already has. Detect a timeout binary at recipe runtime: prefer `timeout`, else `gtimeout` (GNU coreutils name on macOS), else run unwrapped (the --non-interactive change alone already removes the deterministic hang, so absence of `timeout` must NOT break `make test` on a stock macOS host). Use a generous bound consistent with trunk/run-load-and-test-all.sh's 1800s for sento. When the timeout binary fires (exit code 124), print a clear `=== FAIL: host-cold-test timed out after <N>s (see $(HOST_COLD_TEST_LOG)) ===`, tail the log, and exit 1. Preserve all existing post-run checks (FATAL Signal grep, the `Did [0-9]+ checks` result-line grep, and the rc check) and the existing PASS summary line.

REGRESSION TEST (tests/test_load_exit.sh, new; CLAUDE.md requires one): a dependency-free shell test that pins the no-hang guarantees the cold-test now relies on. Model style/skeleton on tests/test_batch.sh (CLAMIGA="${1:-build/host/clamiga}", check helper, pass/fail tally, exit 1 on failure). It must:
  - Skip cleanly (echo a SKIP line, exit 0) if neither `timeout` nor `gtimeout` is on PATH, matching the repo's auto-skip-when-prereq-missing convention.
  - Create a tiny temp Lisp script (e.g. `(format t "COLD-LOAD-DONE~%")`) in a mktemp file; clean it up with a trap.
  - Assert A (non-interactive must not block on a stdin that never closes): run `sleep 12 | <TIMEOUT> 10 "$CLAMIGA" --heap 8M --non-interactive --load $tmp` and assert it exits with status 0 (NOT 124) and the output contains COLD-LOAD-DONE. On a regression where --non-interactive starts a REPL, this would time out (124) and fail.
  - Assert B (the `</dev/null` convention exits cleanly): run `<TIMEOUT> 10 "$CLAMIGA" --heap 8M --load $tmp </dev/null` and assert status 0 (NOT 124) and output contains COLD-LOAD-DONE. Guards that an EOF on stdin makes the post-load REPL exit.
  - Use a heap small enough to start fast (8M) since these scripts do no heavy work.

WIRE-UP (Makefile, test-fast recipe, around lines 95-129): add a `--- test_load_exit ---` stanza that runs `sh $(TEST_SRCDIR)/test_load_exit.sh $(BUILDDIR)/clamiga`, following the exact pattern of the existing test_batch / test_fasl_compat / test_boot_fasl_recovery stanzas (set failed=1 on failure). This keeps the regression test in the fast tier so it runs on every `make test` without needing quicklisp.

DO NOT modify trunk/load-and-test-sento-system.lisp or any other trunk script (the no-(quit) / caller-closes-stdin convention is intentional and shared with run-load-and-test-all.sh). Verify `make host` still builds and the new shell test passes; `make test-fast` must stay green.
